title: Atomic guarded update (the winning case)
prompt_md:
The safest way to change data under concurrency is to fold the **check** and the **change** into one statement. Update the `rating` of the movie with `id` `3` (Tenet) to `9.0`, but **only if** its current rating is below `9.0`. Return the `title` and new `rating`.

Because this runs as one indivisible step, two requests can't both pass the check.
starter_code: UPDATE movies SET rating = 9.0 WHERE id = 3 AND ... RETURNING title, rating;
hints: Add a guard to the WHERE: `id = 3 AND rating < 9.0`.
Tenet starts at 7.4, so the guard passes and one row is updated.
reference_solution: UPDATE movies SET rating = 9.0 WHERE id = 3 AND rating < 9.0 RETURNING title, rating;
